item: Never call the pricing API without a circuit breaker; an outage there took down checkout for 40 minutes because every request retried in a tight loop.
context: Review comment on PR 7781; the author added a Polly circuit breaker and the merged diff shows it wrapping the pricing client.
note: Durable, rationalized, evidenced, adopted in merged code.

item: The `orders` event hub is owned by the Fulfillment team and has exactly one consumer group; coordinate before changing partition keys.
context: Stated by the Fulfillment lead in a design review. No diff can prove the downstream impact.
note: Useful world fact with a named attestor -> human-attested route.

item: Change this variable name on line 42 to `userId`.
context: Review comment whose only meaning is to rename one local identifier in one place.
note: Means only 'change this exact line here'.

item: We should make this cleaner and improve maintainability.
context: Review comment with no project-specific rationale or future check.
note: Generic quality phrase, no concrete future check.

item: Always seed deterministic clocks in time-sensitive tests; wall-clock reads flake around midnight and across timezones.
context: PR 4490 description states the rule and the merged test injects a FakeClock.
note: Rule + why stated in PR description and corroborated by merged code.

item: Reviewer asked to drop the feature flag, but the team decided to keep it; merged code still has the flag.
context: Review comment overruled by the merged code.
note: Overruled / contradicted by merged code -> not adopted.

item: Fixed the typo in the log message.
context: PR 7801, a one-off cleanup with no generalizable rule.
note: One-off cleanup, not durable or generalizable.

context: A vague preference with no why and no evidence of adoption anywhere.
note: No why, no application check, no evidence.

item: Never remove a public API field without a one-release deprecation window; two partners read `legacyId` and would break silently.
context: Review comment on PR 4821; merged diff keeps the field serialized behind an Obsolete deprecation flag.
note: Durable compatibility rule with rationale and merged adoption proof.

item: The CI image must stay on Ubuntu 22.04 because the GPU driver build breaks on 24.04; we burned two days on this.
context: Captured by hand via /greybeard:remember; attested by the build owner, forward-looking pin with named source.
note: Operational gotcha no diff proves; named attestor -> human-attested.
Status noise with no specific future check.
